---
title: "Theorem 3.5 — Frisch–Waugh–Lovell"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.fromColsRightBeta_eq_fwlBeta"
chapter: 3
---

## Textbook statement

Partition the regressors $X = [X_1, X_2]$ with $k_1$ and $k_2$ columns
respectively, and consider the full regression

$$
y = X_1 \hat\beta_1 + X_2 \hat\beta_2 + \hat e.
$$

Let $M_1 = I - X_1(X_1^\top X_1)^{-1} X_1^\top$ be the annihilator for $X_1$,
and define the **residualized** quantities $\tilde y = M_1 y$ and
$\tilde X_2 = M_1 X_2$.

The **Frisch–Waugh–Lovell theorem** states:

1. **Coefficient part.** The second-block coefficient $\hat\beta_2$ from the
   full regression equals the coefficient from the auxiliary OLS of
   $\tilde y$ on $\tilde X_2$:
   $$
   \hat\beta_2 = (\tilde X_2^\top \tilde X_2)^{-1} \tilde X_2^\top \tilde y.
   $$
2. **Residual part.** The residuals from the auxiliary regression equal the
   residuals from the full regression:
   $$
   \tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2 = \hat e.
   $$

For partitioned design matrices $X_1 \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k_1}$ and
$X_2 \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k_2}$ and response vector $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$,
the formalized coefficient theorem assumes three nonsingularity witnesses:
[$X_1^\top X_1$, $[X_1,X_2]^\top[X_1,X_2]$, and
$(M_1X_2)^\top(M_1X_2)$ are invertible](../../assumptions/chapter3.qmd). No
distributional assumption is needed; FWL is purely algebraic.

So you can recover the partial effect $\hat\beta_2$ — and the same residuals
— by first partialling out $X_1$ from both $y$ and $X_2$, then running OLS on
the residualized variables.

### Proof sketch

The argument has two halves.

**Coefficient part.** Show that the full-regression coefficient
$\hat\beta_2$ satisfies the auxiliary normal equations

$$
\tilde X_2^\top (\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2) = 0,
$$

then invoke uniqueness of the OLS solution.

To verify the auxiliary normal equations, decompose
$\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2$. Substitute $\tilde y = M_1 y$ and
$\tilde X_2 = M_1 X_2$, and use $\hat e = y - X_1 \hat\beta_1 - X_2 \hat\beta_2$
from the full regression. After algebra,

$$
\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2 = M_1 \hat e.
$$

Now use $X_1^\top \hat e = 0$ (normal equations for the $X_1$ block of the
full regression) to get $M_1 \hat e = \hat e$, so
$\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2 = \hat e$. Premultiplying by
$\tilde X_2^\top = X_2^\top M_1$ and using $X_2^\top \hat e = 0$ (the other
block's normal equations) gives the auxiliary normal equations. Uniqueness
then implies $\hat\beta_2$ is the auxiliary regression's coefficient.

**Residual part.** The chain above already showed
$\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2 = \hat e$, which is exactly the residual
identity once you observe that the auxiliary regression's residual at the FWL
coefficient is $\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2^{\text{FWL}}$ and
$\hat\beta_2^{\text{FWL}} = \hat\beta_2$ by the coefficient part.

## Lean statement

All in `HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean`:

- [`residualizedRegressors`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L51)
  at `Chapter3FWL.lean:51` — defined as `annihilatorMatrix X₁ * X₂`, i.e.
  $\tilde X_2 = M_1 X_2$.
- [`fwlBeta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L58)
  at `Chapter3FWL.lean:58` — OLS coefficient from regressing $\tilde y = M_1 y$
  on $\tilde X_2$.
- [`fromColsRightBeta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L75)
  at `Chapter3FWL.lean:75` — the $X_2$-block of the full-regression
  coefficient on $[X_1, X_2]$.
- [`fromColsRightBeta_eq_fwlBeta`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L147)
  at `Chapter3FWL.lean:147` — coefficient part of FWL.
- [`fwl_residual_eq_full_residual`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L163)
  at `Chapter3FWL.lean:163` — residual part of FWL.

The two headline theorems:

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Coefficient face of FWL: the right block of the full-regression coefficient equals the residualized-regression coefficient.">theorem fromColsRightBeta_eq_fwlBeta</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The two regressor blocks and response vector. Matrix.fromCols glues X₁ and X₂ into the full design.">(X₁ : Matrix n k₁ ℝ) (X₂ : Matrix n k₂ ℝ) (y : n → ℝ)</span>
    [DecidableEq n] [Fintype k₁] [DecidableEq k₁] [Fintype k₂] [DecidableEq k₂]
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="First-block full-rank condition, needed to define the annihilator M₁.">[Invertible (X₁ᵀ * X₁)]</span>
    [Invertible ((Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)ᵀ * Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)]
    [Invertible ((residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)ᵀ * residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)] :
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The conclusion: the full-regression X₂ coefficient equals the auxiliary OLS coefficient on M₁y and M₁X₂.">fromColsRightBeta X₁ X₂ y = fwlBeta X₁ X₂ y</span>

<span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Residual face of FWL: the auxiliary residual equals the full-regression residual.">theorem fwl_residual_eq_full_residual</span>
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="Same partitioned design and response vector.">(X₁ : Matrix n k₁ ℝ) (X₂ : Matrix n k₂ ℝ) (y : n → ℝ)</span>
    [DecidableEq n] [Fintype k₁] [DecidableEq k₁] [Fintype k₂] [DecidableEq k₂]
    [Invertible (X₁ᵀ * X₁)]
    [Invertible ((Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)ᵀ * Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)]
    [Invertible ((residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)ᵀ * residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)] :
    <span class="lean-token" tabindex="0" data-explain="The residual from regressing M₁y on M₁X₂ is exactly the residual from regressing y on [X₁, X₂].">residual (residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂) (annihilatorMatrix X₁ *ᵥ y) =
      residual (Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂) y</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

### Reading the statement

In English (coefficient part): **given** a partitioned design $[X_1, X_2]$, a
response vector $y$, and witnesses that $X_1^\top X_1$, the full Gram matrix,
and the residualized Gram matrix are all invertible, the second-block
coefficient from the full regression equals the OLS coefficient from
regressing $M_1 y$ on $M_1 X_2$.

| Lean | Math | What it gives us |
|---|---|---|
| `(X₁ : Matrix n k₁ ℝ)` | $X_1 \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k_1}$ | **Given:** first block of regressors. The index types `n`, `k₁`, `k₂` come from the file's `variable` block. |
| `(X₂ : Matrix n k₂ ℝ)` | $X_2 \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k_2}$ | **Given:** second block of regressors. Same row index `n` so the two blocks can be column-glued. |
| `(y : n → ℝ)` | $y \in \mathbb{R}^n$ | **Given:** response vector, modeled as a function `n → ℝ`. |
| `[DecidableEq n]` | (decidable equality on $\{1,\ldots,n\}$) | Mathlib bookkeeping: Lean needs to decide row equality to define the annihilator $M_1 = I - X_1(X_1^\top X_1)^{-1}X_1^\top$ (the identity matrix is built using `if i = j`). |
| `[Fintype k₁] [Fintype k₂]` | $k_1, k_2$ finite | Mathlib bookkeeping: column index types are finite, so matrix sums and products are well-defined. |
| `[DecidableEq k₁] [DecidableEq k₂]` | (decidable equality on column indices) | Mathlib bookkeeping: needed for identity matrices on the column spaces and for `fromCols`-style index manipulation. |
| `[Invertible (X₁ᵀ * X₁)]` | $X_1^\top X_1$ invertible | **Given (hypothesis):** first block has full column rank — needed so $M_1$ is well-defined. |
| `[Invertible ((Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)ᵀ * Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)]` | $[X_1,X_2]^\top[X_1,X_2]$ invertible | **Given (hypothesis):** the joint design has full column rank — needed so the full-regression coefficient `olsBeta (fromCols X₁ X₂) y` exists. |
| `[Invertible ((residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)ᵀ * residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)]` | $\tilde X_2^\top \tilde X_2$ invertible | **Given (hypothesis):** the residualized regressors have full column rank — needed so `fwlBeta`, the auxiliary OLS coefficient, exists. |
| `fromColsRightBeta X₁ X₂ y` | $\hat\beta_2$ from the full regression | LHS of the conclusion. Defined as `fun j => olsBeta (fromCols X₁ X₂) y (Sum.inr j)` — projecting the full coefficient vector to its $X_2$ block via `Sum.inr`. |
| `= fwlBeta X₁ X₂ y` | $= (\tilde X_2^\top\tilde X_2)^{-1}\tilde X_2^\top\tilde y$ | **Conclusion:** equality of the two `k₂ → ℝ` vectors. `fwlBeta` is defined as `olsBeta (M₁ X₂) (M₁ y)`. |

(Square brackets mark instance arguments — they are filled in by Lean's
typeclass resolution rather than supplied positionally. The three `Invertible`
instances are the substantive economic/algebraic hypotheses; the
`DecidableEq`/`Fintype` instances are Mathlib boilerplate that lets the
machinery run.)

Supporting / helper theorems used internally:

- [`normal_equations_fromCols_left`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L15)
  / [`normal_equations_fromCols_right`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L26)
  at lines 15 and 26.
- [`regressors_transpose_mul_annihilator`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L37)
  at line 37 — $X^\top M = 0$.
- [`residualizedRegressors_orthogonal_left`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L94)
  at line 94 — $X_1^\top \tilde X_2 = 0$.
- [`fwl_auxiliary_residual_eq_annihilator_full_residual`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L106)
  at line 106 — bridges auxiliary residual to $M_1 \hat e_{\text{full}}$.
- [`fwl_fromColsRightBeta_normal_equations`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L123)
  at line 123 — full-regression $\hat\beta_2$ satisfies the auxiliary normal
  equations.

## Translation notes

- Hansen states FWL with $\hat\beta_2$ from regressing $\tilde y$ on
  $\tilde X_2$; Lean defines `fwlBeta := olsBeta (M_1 X_2) (M_1 y)`.
  Identical.
- Lean uses `Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂` for the column-partitioned $[X_1, X_2]$.
  The full-regression coefficients are projected to their two blocks via
  `Sum.inl` / `Sum.inr` (the index type is `k₁ ⊕ k₂`).
- The residual part of FWL is stated using the auxiliary regression's
  residual; the textbook commonly states
  "$\tilde y - \tilde X_2 \hat\beta_2 = \hat e$" with $\hat\beta_2$ from the
  *full* regression. Lean's `fwl_residual_eq_full_residual` is the
  corresponding equality in the formalization.
- Three invertibility instances are required for the coefficient theorem:
  $X_1^\top X_1$, $[X_1, X_2]^\top [X_1, X_2]$, and
  $\tilde X_2^\top \tilde X_2$. The third is genuinely needed — without it,
  `fwlBeta` is undefined.

## Tactic-by-tactic walkthrough

The headline theorem itself is short — it just packages uniqueness of OLS:

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The coefficient theorem: the X₂ block from the full regression equals the auxiliary OLS coefficient after residualizing y and X₂ against X₁.">theorem fromColsRightBeta_eq_fwlBeta
    (X₁ : Matrix n k₁ ℝ) (X₂ : Matrix n k₂ ℝ) (y : n → ℝ)
    [DecidableEq n] [Fintype k₁] [DecidableEq k₁] [Fintype k₂] [DecidableEq k₂]
    [Invertible (X₁ᵀ * X₁)]
    [Invertible ((Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)ᵀ * Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)]
    [Invertible ((residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)ᵀ * residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)] :
    fromColsRightBeta X₁ X₂ y = fwlBeta X₁ X₂ y := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Flip the goal so it has the shape produced by the OLS uniqueness theorem: auxiliary OLS equals a candidate coefficient vector.">  symm</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Replace fwlBeta by the OLS coefficient from the residualized regression of M₁y on M₁X₂.">  unfold fwlBeta</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Apply the normal-equations uniqueness theorem with design M₁X₂, response M₁y, and candidate coefficient equal to the full-regression X₂ block.">  exact olsBeta_eq_of_normal_equations
    (residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)
    (annihilatorMatrix X₁ *ᵥ y)
    (fromColsRightBeta X₁ X₂ y)
    (fwl_fromColsRightBeta_normal_equations X₁ X₂ y)</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

The substantive work lives in
[`fwl_fromColsRightBeta_normal_equations`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L123)
(line 123). Its proof body:

::: {.lean-hover-panel}
```{=html}
<pre><code><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="The auxiliary normal-equations lemma: the full-regression X₂ coefficient satisfies the residualized regression's normal equations.">theorem fwl_fromColsRightBeta_normal_equations
    (X₁ : Matrix n k₁ ℝ) (X₂ : Matrix n k₂ ℝ) (y : n → ℝ)
    [DecidableEq n] [Fintype k₁] [DecidableEq k₁] [Fintype k₂] [DecidableEq k₂]
    [Invertible (X₁ᵀ * X₁)]
    [Invertible ((Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)ᵀ * Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂)] :
    (residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂)ᵀ *ᵥ
        (annihilatorMatrix X₁ *ᵥ y -
          residualizedRegressors X₁ X₂ *ᵥ fromColsRightBeta X₁ X₂ y) = 0 := by</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Rewrite the auxiliary residual ỹ - X̃₂ β₂(full) as M₁ times the full-regression residual.">  rw [fwl_auxiliary_residual_eq_annihilator_full_residual]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Record that M₁ fixes the full residual, because the full residual is orthogonal to X₁ by the left-block normal equations.">  have hM :
      annihilatorMatrix X₁ *ᵥ residual (Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂) y =
        residual (Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂) y :=
    annihilator_mulVec_eq_self_of_regressors_orthogonal X₁
      (residual (Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂) y)
      (normal_equations_fromCols_left X₁ X₂ y)</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Use hM once to replace M₁ e_full by e_full.">  rw [hM]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Replace residualizedRegressors by its definition M₁X₂.">  unfold residualizedRegressors</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Transpose M₁X₂ as X₂ᵀM₁, using symmetry of the annihilator matrix.">  rw [Matrix.transpose_mul, annihilatorMatrix_transpose]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Re-express (X₂ᵀM₁)e as X₂ᵀ(M₁e), so hM can remove the remaining M₁.">  rw [← Matrix.mulVec_mulVec (residual (Matrix.fromCols X₁ X₂) y) X₂ᵀ
    (annihilatorMatrix X₁)]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Use hM a second time: M₁ fixes the full residual.">  rw [hM]</span><span class="lean-line" tabindex="0" data-explain="Finish with the right-block normal equations for the full regression, X₂ᵀ e_full = 0.">  exact normal_equations_fromCols_right X₁ X₂ y</span>
</code></pre>
```
:::

The shape of the proof is "rewrite using the bridge, kill the $M_1$ on both
sides using $X_1^\top \hat e = 0$, then finish with $X_2^\top \hat e = 0$" —
exactly the algebraic sketch above, broken into seven `rw`/`exact` steps.

## Downstream uses

- [`fwl_normal_equations`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L181)
  (line 181) — straightforward consequence, packaged as the auxiliary
  regression's normal equations.
- [`fwl_residual_maker_mul_fromCols`](https://github.com/apoorvalal/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean#L195)
  (line 195) — the sequential residual-maker $M_{\tilde X_2} M_1$ kills both
  $X_1$ and $X_2$, used in projection-geometry arguments downstream.
- (Future) Chapter 4 / 7 partial-effect arguments will likely consume FWL —
  not yet wired up at the time of writing.
